name: Tomato

identity.canonicalName: Tomatoes, red, ripe, raw, year round average
identity.ediblePortion: edible portion with skin and flesh
identity.basisReason: Raw per-100g edible-portion basis matches the actual base food being ranked.

metricProvenance.header: USDA FoodData Central style raw red-ripe-tomato entry, normalized to per 100 g edible portion
metricProvenance.saturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.omega3_mg: derived trace fatty-acid field from the same source family; low importance for vegetable ranking
metricProvenance.polyunsaturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.cholesterol_mg: plant food default, canonical expectation confirmed by source family
metricProvenance.starch_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.fibre_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.sugar_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.glycemic_index: reasonable canonical low-GI value for raw tomato; still less central than fibre and micronutrients
metricProvenance.essential_amino_acids_score: withheld, proxy score not useful enough to justify for this vegetable entry
metricProvenance.nonessential_amino_acids_score: withheld, proxy score not useful enough to justify for this vegetable entry
metricProvenance.bioavailability_percent: withheld, proxy estimate not production-safe for this vegetable entry

pros.title: tomatoes bring a distinctive lycopene story
pros.explanation: That antioxidant identity is a more tomato-specific strength than generic low-calorie praise.

pros.title: high water content gives a lot of food volume for few calories
pros.explanation: Tomatoes are useful for adding bulk and freshness without much calorie cost.

pros.title: works raw or cooked in a lot of meals
pros.explanation: That versatility is one reason tomatoes are easy to use regularly rather than just admire nutritionally.

cons.title: it contributes freshness more than serious energy or protein
cons.explanation: A tomato can improve a meal, but it usually does not do the heavy lifting by itself.

cons.title: acidity can be rough for some people
cons.explanation: Tomatoes can aggravate reflux or sensitivity in a way that is specific enough to mention.

cons.title: flavor and quality swing a lot with ripeness and season
cons.explanation: That does not erase the nutrition case, but it does affect real-world usefulness and appeal.

scoreReadiness.summary: Identity, basis, and core nutrient fields are coherent enough for production use. Weak protein proxy metrics were removed, but the remaining tomato record is honest and stable.

sourceNotes: Identity locked to raw red ripe tomato, year-round average, per 100 g edible portion.
Core nutrient values align with common USDA FoodData Central style entries for raw tomato.
Weak proxy metrics were removed rather than preserved as fake certainty.
